steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank completes "an ecological ______ compared the lengths of eggs..." The verb phrase "compared the lengths of eggs produced by different bird species" must attach to the noun in the blank. That attachment requires a relative pronoun forming a restrictive relative clause — one that identifies which study is being described.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘study, that’: a comma before that is non-standard. Restrictive relative clauses with that never take a preceding comma. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘study’: omits the relative pronoun entirely, leaving "an ecological study compared the lengths..." — "study" becomes the subject of the main clause, and "compared" then functions as the main verb, but the sentence already has no other subject or connector, creating a run-on with the earlier clause. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘study that’: that introduces a restrictive relative clause with no comma — grammatically correct and natural. "An ecological study that compared...found..." is well-formed. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘study, which’: a comma + which introduces a non-restrictive (parenthetical) clause. Non-restrictive clauses add bonus information about something already identified; here the study is not yet identified, so a restrictive clause is required. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
